skill.name: ai-sdk
skill.description: Helps developers use the Vercel AI SDK with current docs, type-safe agents, streaming chat, structured output, tool calling, embeddings, and AI Gateway model selection.
skill.summary: Build Vercel AI SDK features using current local docs and safe implementation patterns.
skill.author: vercel

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, filesystem, and secret patterns, but most are Markdown examples or inline API names rather than executable skill code. The skill does intentionally direct assistants to install the ai package, inspect node_modules, read package.json, and call Vercel AI Gateway, so publication should include a medium-risk warning about package installation and network access.

medium_findings.title: Package Installation and External Documentation Commands
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE. The skill instructs assistants to install the ai package if local docs are missing, search dependency folders, and run curl against Vercel AI Gateway for current model IDs. This behavior is legitimate for an AI SDK helper, but it performs package-manager and network operations in user projects. Confidence: 0.78. Confidence reasoning: The commands are explicit in the skill guidance and reference docs, but the target package and endpoint are Vercel AI SDK resources rather than suspicious third-party infrastructure.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit in the skill guidance and reference docs, but the target package and endpoint are Vercel AI SDK resources rather than suspicious third-party infrastructure.

medium_findings.title: Local Project and Dependency Inspection
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE with limited scope. The skill instructs assistants to inspect node_modules, provider documentation folders, source files, and package.json to determine current APIs and framework patterns. This is normal coding-assistant behavior, but it requires filesystem reads in the user project. Confidence: 0.72. Confidence reasoning: The filesystem access instructions are clear, yet they are limited to dependency documentation, source references, and project metadata needed for implementation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem access instructions are clear, yet they are limited to dependency documentation, source references, and project metadata needed for implementation.

low_findings.title: Environment Secret Documentation Example
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for credential exfiltration. The detected API key content is an .env.local documentation example for configuring AI Gateway authentication, not code that reads or transmits an existing secret. Users should still treat generated .env guidance as sensitive. Confidence: 0.86. Confidence reasoning: The location is a fenced env example with a placeholder value, and no instruction sends the key to an unauthorized endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The location is a fenced env example with a placeholder value, and no instruction sends the key to an unauthorized endpoint.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fence and Inline Backtick Noise
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for Ruby or shell backtick execution. The numerous backtick findings occur in Markdown headings, inline API identifiers, and fenced TypeScript or shell examples, not executable Ruby code. Confidence: 0.93. Confidence reasoning: Manual review shows the affected files are documentation references, and the backticks delimit Markdown code spans or examples.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows the affected files are documentation references, and the backticks delimit Markdown code spans or examples.

low_findings.title: Relative Import Paths Misclassified as Traversal
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for path traversal. The ../tools imports are TypeScript module examples showing normal project structure, not file reads using user-controlled paths. Confidence: 0.9. Confidence reasoning: The relative paths appear inside static TypeScript import statements and have no dynamic input or filesystem API call.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relative paths appear inside static TypeScript import statements and have no dynamic input or filesystem API call.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Pattern Misclassification
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for weak cryptographic algorithm use. Review found no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other cryptographic implementation; the flagged lines are frontmatter, SDK terminology, or normal AI SDK examples. Confidence: 0.88. Confidence reasoning: The cited files contain documentation prose and TypeScript examples, and no cryptographic API usage was found at the cited contexts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited files contain documentation prose and TypeScript examples, and no cryptographic API usage was found at the cited contexts.

content.value_statement: AI SDK APIs change quickly, which can cause broken chat, agent, and tool code. This skill checks current docs and source references before helping implement Vercel AI SDK features.
actual_capabilities: Guides use of current AI SDK APIs such as generateText, streamText, tools, embeddings, and structured output.
Checks bundled ai package docs and source before relying on implementation details.
Helps build chat interfaces with useChat and current stream response patterns.
Recommends AI Gateway model selection and explains how to fetch current model IDs.
Provides references for type-safe ToolLoopAgent usage and typed UI message rendering.
Maps common deprecated API names to current AI SDK alternatives.
limitations: May install the ai package when local documentation is missing.
May require network access to query AI SDK documentation or AI Gateway model lists.
Does not replace provider account setup, API keys, billing, or deployment configuration.
Generated code still needs project-specific typechecking and runtime testing.

use_cases.title: Add AI Text Generation
use_cases.description: Implement generateText or streamText with current AI SDK options and minimal configuration.

use_cases.title: Fix AI SDK Migration Errors
use_cases.description: Update deprecated parameters, stream responses, and useChat patterns after an AI SDK upgrade.

use_cases.title: Build Type-Safe Agents
use_cases.description: Create ToolLoopAgent workflows with typed tools and UI message rendering for React clients.

prompt_templates.title: Explain an AI SDK API
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain how to use generateText in the current AI SDK. Check local AI SDK docs first and mention any version-specific caveats.

prompt_templates.title: Add Streaming Chat
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a streaming chat route and React client using the current AI SDK useChat pattern. Run typecheck after the change.

prompt_templates.title: Migrate Deprecated Usage
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this AI SDK code for deprecated options and update it to current APIs. Explain each behavior change briefly.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Typed Agent Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a ToolLoopAgent with typed tools and a type-safe UI message renderer. Verify model IDs and avoid unsupported AI SDK APIs.

output_examples.input: Help me add an AI SDK chat UI to my app.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies the app framework, checks current AI SDK docs, creates a streaming route, wires the React chat hook, and lists typecheck results.

output_examples.input: My AI SDK upgrade broke useChat and tool rendering.
output: Deprecated hook fields are replaced with current state handling.
Tool result rendering is updated to typed tool parts.
The assistant explains the migration and remaining test steps.

output_examples.input: Create a type-safe agent that uses weather and calculator tools.
output_examples.output: The assistant proposes file organization, tool schemas, agent setup, inferred UI message types, and component rendering checks.

best_practices: Run the project typecheck after AI SDK code changes.
Verify model IDs from current docs or AI Gateway before writing provider configuration.
Keep tool schemas explicit so agent outputs and UI rendering stay type-safe.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on remembered AI SDK APIs without checking local docs or source.
Do not add provider packages before confirming the selected provider and project requirements.
Do not render tool outputs before checking their current streaming state.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps developers build, debug, and migrate Vercel AI SDK features using current docs and source references.

faq.question: Does it support Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Will it install packages?
faq.answer: It may install only the ai package when local AI SDK documentation is missing.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: It may query ai-sdk.dev or Vercel AI Gateway to verify current documentation and model IDs.

faq.question: Can it build production agents?
faq.answer: It can guide ToolLoopAgent setup and type-safe UI rendering, but deployment, billing, and provider credentials remain project responsibilities.

faq.question: Why is the risk level medium?
faq.answer: The skill has legitimate external commands, package installation guidance, filesystem reads, and network requests, but no malicious behavior was found.
